author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: The Great Gatsby is a classic novel set in the Jazz Age, exploring themes of wealth, love, and ambition. It follows the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his obsession with Daisy Buchanan. Through the eyes of Nick Carraway, the story reveals the emptiness behind glamorous lifestyles. The novel beautifully portrays the illusion of the American Dream. It remains one of the most powerful literary works of modern fiction.

author: Harper Lee
description: This novel tells the story of racial injustice in a small Southern town. Through the perspective of young Scout Finch, readers witness moral growth and social inequality. Her father, Atticus Finch, defends a wrongly accused man. The story highlights empathy, justice, and human dignity. It is a deeply moving literary masterpiece.

author: George Orwell
description: 1984 is a dystopian novel about a world under constant surveillance. The government controls truth, history, and freedom. Winston Smith struggles to maintain his individuality in a controlled society. The story warns about totalitarianism and loss of freedom. It remains highly relevant in modern politics.

author: J.D. Salinger
description: The story follows Holden Caulfield, a teenager struggling with identity and adulthood. After leaving school, he wanders through New York City. He feels disconnected from the adult world. The novel captures teenage confusion and rebellion. It is a powerful coming-of-age story.

author: Jane Austen
description: A romantic novel about Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy. It explores love, pride, and social class. Misunderstandings shape their relationship journey. The story highlights personal growth and judgment. It is one of the greatest classic romances.

author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Bilbo Baggins joins a quest to reclaim treasure guarded by a dragon. The journey is full of danger, magic, and discovery. He grows from a simple hobbit into a brave adventurer. The story builds the foundation of fantasy literature. It is a classic adventure tale.

bookName: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry discovers he is a wizard and joins Hogwarts. He learns magic, friendship, and bravery. He uncovers secrets about his past and destiny. The world of magic opens a new adventure for him. It begins a globally loved series.

author: Paulo Coelho
description: A shepherd named Santiago follows his dream of finding treasure. His journey teaches him about destiny and purpose. He travels across deserts and faces challenges. The story emphasizes following your heart. It is a philosophical inspirational novel.

author: Paula Hawkins
description: A woman becomes involved in a missing person investigation. She observes lives from a train every day. Memory gaps and secrets create suspense. The story unfolds through multiple perspectives. It is a gripping psychological thriller.

author: Robert C. Martin
description: A guide for writing clean and professional code. It teaches best practices for software development. Developers learn how to structure readable programs. It emphasizes simplicity and maintainability. A must-read for programmers.

author: James Clear
description: This book explains how small habits create big changes. It focuses on behavior improvement and consistency. Readers learn practical strategies for growth. It uses science-backed methods for habit building. It is highly useful for self-improvement.

author: Kyle Simpson
description: This book deeply explains JavaScript internals. It covers scope, closures, and advanced concepts. Developers gain deeper understanding of how JS works. It is designed for serious programmers. It is essential for mastering JavaScript.
